exam.name: Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) Certification Exam
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for project management certifications with structured coverage of leadership, delivery, governance, and business outcomes.
exam.hero_headline: Pass the CAPM with official-outline coverage.
exam.hero_subheadline: Study the PMI exam content in focused 15-minute topics that build confidence across fundamentals, predictive delivery, agile methods, and business analysis.
exam.companion_problem: Most CAPM prep resources either bury beginners in jargon or skip around the official outline, making it hard to build confidence and retain the core concepts the exam actually tests.
exam.companion_solution: Only Ever turns the official PMI exam content outline into a clear sequence of 15-minute study topics, so you can learn the fundamentals, methodologies, and business analysis concepts in a structured way.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring project coordinator, project specialist, operations professional, student, or career switcher building foundational project management knowledge. Benefits from clear explanations, exam-aligned sequencing, and practical distinctions between predictive, agile, and business analysis concepts.

sections.title: Project Management Fundamentals and Core Concepts
sections.description: Understand foundational project management topics including project life cycles, planning processes, core roles, strategies, and problem-solving techniques.

topics.title: Project, Program, and Portfolio Management
topics.description: Distinguish between the different levels of organizational management and understand the differences between projects and ongoing operations.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between a project, program, and a portfolio.
Distinguish between a project and operations.
Explain how a project can be a vehicle for change.

topics.title: Project Life Cycles and Approaches
topics.description: Learn the characteristics of different project approaches, primarily contrasting predictive (waterfall) and adaptive (agile) life cycles.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate an understanding of various project life cycles and processes.
Distinguish between predictive and adaptive approaches.

topics.title: Scope, Ethics, and Constraints
topics.description: Understand core project constraints, review project scope, and apply the PMI Code of Ethics.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between issues, risks, assumptions, and constraints.
Review and critique project scope.
Apply the project management code of ethics to scenarios.

topics.title: Project Management Planning Basics
topics.description: Introduction to project management planning, emphasizing cost, quality, risk, schedule, and plan deliverables.
learning_objectives: Describe the purpose and importance of cost, quality, risk, and schedule.
Distinguish between the deliverables of a project management plan versus a product management plan.

topics.title: Milestones, Task Durations, and Resources
topics.description: Learn the difference between milestones and general task durations, and determine project resource needs.
learning_objectives: Distinguish differences between a milestone and a task duration.
Determine the number and type of resources in a project.

topics.title: Registers and Project Closure
topics.description: Understand how to utilize risk and stakeholder registers and how to execute project closure and transitions.
learning_objectives: Use a risk register in a given situation.
Use a stakeholder register in a given situation.
Explain project closure and transitions.

topics.title: Project Roles and Responsibilities
topics.description: Differentiate between the distinct roles of the project manager, project team, and the project sponsor.
learning_objectives: Compare and contrast the roles and responsibilities of project managers and project sponsors.
Compare and contrast the roles and responsibilities of the project team and the project sponsor.
Explain the importance of the role the project manager plays (e.g., initiator, negotiator, listener, coach, working member, and facilitator).

topics.title: Leadership, Management, and Emotional Intelligence
topics.description: Explore the critical differences between leadership and management, and the impact of emotional intelligence.
learning_objectives: Explain the differences between leadership and management.
Explain emotional intelligence (EQ) and its impact on project management.

topics.title: Planned Strategies and Frameworks
topics.description: Determine how to properly follow and execute communication, risk, and benefit planning strategies.
learning_objectives: Give examples of how it is appropriate to respond to a planned strategy or framework (e.g., communication, risk, etc.).
Explain project initiation and benefit planning.

topics.title: Problem-Solving Tools and Meetings
topics.description: A focus on evaluating meeting effectiveness and utilizing specific tools such as focus groups and brainstorming.
learning_objectives: Evaluate the effectiveness of a meeting.
Explain the purpose of focus groups, standup meetings, brainstorming, etc.

sections.title: Predictive, Plan-Based Methodologies
sections.description: Delve into traditional plan-based approaches, exploring when to use predictive methodologies, how to plan schedules, and how to enact project controls.

topics.title: Predictive Approach Suitability
topics.description: Understand the environments and organizational structures where a predictive (waterfall) methodology is appropriate.
learning_objectives: Identify the suitability of a predictive, plan-based approach for the organizational structure (e.g., virtual, colocation, matrix structure, hierarchical, etc.).
Distinguish the differences between various project components in a predictive approach.

topics.title: Predictive Processes and Activities
topics.description: Break down the typical activities that take place across different project management processes.
learning_objectives: Determine the activities within each process.
Give examples of typical activities within each process.

topics.title: Schedule Management and Critical Path
topics.description: Learn to manage and measure project schedules using critical path methods and schedule variance calculations.
learning_objectives: Apply critical path methods.
Calculate schedule variance.

topics.title: WBS and Work Packages
topics.description: Explore the components of project scope breakdown in predictive methodologies.
learning_objectives: Explain work breakdown structures (WBS).
Explain work packages.

topics.title: Quality and Integration Management
topics.description: Apply quality standards and ensure overall integration management across the project lifecycle.
learning_objectives: Apply a quality management plan.
Apply an integration management plan.

topics.title: Predictive Project Controls
topics.description: Discover how to document project controls, calculate project variance, and utilize standard predictive artifacts.
learning_objectives: Identify artifacts that are used in predictive, plan-based projects.
Calculate cost and schedule variances.

sections.title: Agile Frameworks/Methodologies
sections.description: Examine adaptive and agile project management practices, from assessing suitability and tracking iterations to managing tasks.

topics.title: Adaptive Approach Suitability
topics.description: Understand the pros and cons of adaptive methodologies and their alignment with organizational structures and assets.
learning_objectives: Compare the pros and cons of adaptive and predictive, plan-based projects.
Identify the suitability of adaptive approaches for the organizational structure.
Identify organizational process assets and enterprise environmental factors that facilitate the use of adaptive approaches.

topics.title: Planning Project Iterations
topics.description: Gain insight into logical units of agile iterations and transitioning a predictive WBS into an adaptive structure.
learning_objectives: Distinguish the logical units of iterations.
Interpret the pros and cons of the iteration.
Translate a WBS to an adaptive iteration.

topics.title: Adaptive Scope and Tracking
topics.description: Explore how scope is defined in agile formats and compare adaptive tracking techniques to predictive tracking.
learning_objectives: Determine inputs for scope.
Explain the importance of adaptive project tracking versus predictive, plan-based tracking.

topics.title: Adaptive Project Controls and Artifacts
topics.description: Identify common artifacts such as burndown charts and product backlogs used for project control in agile frameworks.
learning_objectives: Determine how to document project controls for an adaptive project.
Identify artifacts that are used in adaptive projects.

topics.title: Adaptive Plan Components and Methodologies
topics.description: Learn to differentiate between specific agile frameworks such as Scrum, XP, SAFe, and Kanban.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between the components of different adaptive methodologies (e.g., Scrum, Extreme Programming (XP), Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe), Kanban, etc.).

topics.title: Adaptive Task Management
topics.description: Focus on the execution and prioritization of tasks within agile delivery environments.
learning_objectives: Determine how to prepare and execute task management steps.
Interpret success criteria of an adaptive project management task.
Prioritize tasks in adaptive project management.

sections.title: Business Analysis Frameworks
sections.description: Review the essential role of business analysis in project success, encompassing stakeholder communication, requirements gathering, and product roadmaps.

topics.title: Business Analysis Roles and Responsibilities
topics.description: Recognize the varied stakeholder roles and differentiate between internal and external roles within business analysis.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between stakeholder roles (e.g., process owner, process manager, product manager, product owner).
Outline the need for identifying roles and responsibilities in the first place.
Differentiate between internal and external roles.

topics.title: Stakeholder Communication in Business Analysis
topics.description: Learn to formulate effective communication plans and select proper channels to connect business teams with development goals.
learning_objectives: Recommend the most appropriate communication channel/tool (e.g., reporting, presentation).
Demonstrate why communication is important for a business analyst between various teams.

topics.title: Requirements Gathering Tools and Approaches
topics.description: Discover various techniques, such as interviews, surveys, and use cases, employed to successfully extract project requirements.
learning_objectives: Match requirements gathering tools to scenarios (e.g., user stories, use cases).
Identify the requirements gathering approach for a situation (e.g., interviews, surveys, workshops, lessons learned).

topics.title: Requirements Traceability and Product Backlog
topics.description: Understand how to trace gathered requirements and properly construct and manage a product backlog.
learning_objectives: Explain a requirements traceability matrix.
Explain a product backlog.

topics.title: Product Roadmaps and Releases
topics.description: Understand the layout and significance of product roadmaps and map components to respective product releases.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate an understanding of product roadmaps and explain their application.
Determine which components go to which releases.

topics.title: Methodologies' Influence on Business Analysis
topics.description: Investigate how the adoption of predictive versus adaptive methodologies shifts the day-to-day operations of a business analyst.
learning_objectives: Determine how project methodologies influence business analysis processes.
Determine the role of a business analyst in adaptive and/or predictive, plan-based approaches.

topics.title: Validating Requirements Through Delivery
topics.description: Define success by validating requirements, setting acceptance criteria, and ensuring delivery readiness.
learning_objectives: Define acceptance criteria (the action of defining changes based on the situation).
Determine if a project/product is ready for delivery based on a requirements traceability matrix/product backlog.
